title: Add Author Information Section to README
description: Create a dedicated author section in the README.md file with proper formatting and relevant information about the project maintainer
details: Locate the existing README.md file in the project root and add an 'Author' section. Include the author's name (rafiulm based on the repository name), optionally add contact information like GitHub profile link, email, or social media handles. Use proper Markdown formatting with appropriate heading level (## Author) and consider adding a brief bio or description of the author's role in the project. Position this section logically within the README structure, typically near the end before license or contributing sections.
test_strategy: Verify the README renders correctly in GitHub's markdown preview, check that all links are functional, and ensure the formatting is consistent with the rest of the document

title: Update Package.json Author Field
description: Add or update the author field in package.json files to maintain consistency across the project configuration
details: Update the main package.json file in the project root to include an 'author' field with the maintainer's information. Follow npm's standard format for author field which can be either a string 'Name <email> (url)' or an object with name, email, and url properties. If the project uses a monorepo structure with multiple package.json files (in apps/frontend and apps/api), ensure consistency across all package.json files. This helps with npm package metadata and project identification.
test_strategy: Validate package.json syntax using npm or yarn commands, verify that the author information displays correctly when running 'npm info' or similar package inspection commands
